phone_1.text: Malik's phone buzzes against the desk. 11:47 PM. Three unread from Ty.

phone_2.speaker: Ty (texts)
phone_2.text:
"yo malik. u up?"
"big tings tonight ahlie"
"pull up to the lot behind the building. 10 mins. dont be a wasteman"

choices.text: Leave him on read. Not tonight.

phone_yes.speaker: Malik
phone_yes.text: Malik pulls his hoodie off the chair. Ma's asleep. Baba's on nights at the warehouse until six. Ten minutes. Whatever Ty's got going in that lot, it starts tonight.

phone_no.speaker: Malik
phone_no.text: He flips the phone face-down. Ty's gonna chirp him about it tomorrow — 'man's got school in the morning' — like that's ever stopped anybody around here. Still. Not tonight.

window_1.text: Fourteen floors up. Jane and Finch spread out under the sodium lights — the towers across the lot, the plaza, the 36 hissing down toward Finch station. Somewhere past all of it, downtown glows like it's a different city. From up here you can see everything Malik wants and every way there is to lose it.

parents_1.text: Ma and Baba's room. Door's shut. Baba's on night shift; Ma fell asleep with the recitation still playing off her phone, same as every night. Keep it quiet.

bathroom_1.text: The bathroom. Somebody's towel is on the floor again. It's Malik's. It's always Malik's.

front_1.speaker: Malik
front_1.text: The front door. Eighteen years coming in and out of this door with groceries, school bags, excuses. Once he starts stepping out at midnight, that's a choice that doesn't un-choose itself.

front_2.text:
— END OF TEST SCENE — 
Act 1, Scene 1: "The Lot" is coming next. This is where Malik's story really starts.
